name: Yggdrasil itself (the cosmic tree)
shortDescription: Yggdrasil is the immense and sacred tree at the center of the Norse cosmos, connecting the nine realms. It is a source of wisdom, life, and connection between gods, humans, and the underworld.
description: Yggdrasil is the central cosmic tree in Norse mythology, an immense ash tree that connects the nine realms of existence. Its roots delve deep into the realms of Hel, Jotunheim, and Niflheim, while its branches reach up to Asgard, the home of the gods. The tree is a constant source of sustenance and connection, though it is also perpetually under assault. The dragon Nidhogg gnaws at its roots, while the squirrel Ratatosk carries insults between Nidhogg and the eagle perched atop its branches. The well of Urd, located at the base of Yggdrasil, is where the Norns weave the threads of fate for all beings. The goat Heidrun stands in Valhalla and feeds on the leaves of Yggdrasil, producing mead for the einherjar. Yggdrasil represents the interconnectedness of all things, the cyclical nature of life and death, and the enduring struggle between order and chaos. It is not merely a tree, but a living embodiment of the cosmos itself, a source of wisdom, power, and the very fabric of reality.
appearance: A colossal ash tree with roots reaching into the depths of various realms and branches extending to the heavens. Described as evergreen and perpetually sustaining the cosmos.
